questions.q: What does the technique of playback singing in Indian cinema refer to?
options: Actors singing live on set during filming
Songs pre-recorded by singers and lip-synced by actors on screen
Background instrumental music played without vocals
Audiences singing along during film screenings
questions.explanation: Playback singing is the technique by which songs are pre-recorded by professional singers and then lip-synced by actors on screen.

questions.q: In which year was playback singing introduced in Indian cinema with the film Dhoop Chhaon?
questions.explanation: Playback singing was introduced in Indian cinema in 1935 with the film Dhoop Chhaon (Bhagya Chakra).

questions.q: The 1935 film credited with introducing playback singing in Indian cinema is known by which Hindi title?
questions.explanation: Dhoop Chhaon, the Hindi version of the Bengali film Bhagya Chakra, introduced playback singing in 1935.

questions.q: Which legendary singer, often called the 'Nightingale of India', became one of the most prolific playback singers in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar, known as the 'Nightingale of India', became one of the most prolific and celebrated playback singers in Indian cinema.

questions.q: Which celebrated Indian playback singer was also a successful actor, music director and producer, starring in comedies like Chalti Ka Naam Gaadi?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar was a celebrated playback singer who was also a successful actor, composer and producer, and starred in films such as Chalti Ka Naam Gaadi.

questions.q: Before playback singing existed, how did actors in early Indian sound films deliver songs?
options: They mimed to records made by other singers
Songs were added silently with subtitles
They sang the songs themselves live while being filmed
An orchestra performed without any vocals
questions.explanation: Before playback singing, actors had to sing their own songs live during filming, which limited casting to those who could both act and sing.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle, a famous Indian playback singer, is the younger sister of which other legendary playback singer?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle is the younger sister of Lata Mangeshkar, and both became iconic playback singers.

questions.q: Which of these is a true description of a 'playback singer' in Indian cinema?
options: A singer who records songs that actors lip-sync on screen
A singer who performs only at live concerts, never for films
An actor who refuses to sing in films
A musician who plays background instruments only
questions.explanation: A playback singer is a professional who records songs in advance that on-screen actors then lip-sync.

questions.q: Playback singing gave rise to a new dedicated profession in Indian cinema. What is that profession called?
questions.explanation: Playback singing created the dedicated profession of the playback singer, separate from acting.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi was famous in Indian cinema primarily for which role?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi was one of the most renowned male playback singers in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Dhoop Chhaon (1935), which introduced playback singing, was produced by which Calcutta-based film studio?
questions.explanation: Dhoop Chhaon was produced by New Theatres of Calcutta, the studio credited with pioneering playback singing in India.

questions.q: Which music director is widely credited with pioneering the playback singing technique in the 1935 film Dhoop Chhaon?
questions.explanation: Rai Chand Boral (R. C. Boral), the music director at New Theatres, is widely credited with pioneering the playback technique used in Dhoop Chhaon.

questions.q: Who directed the 1935 film Dhoop Chhaon (Bhagya Chakra), in which playback singing was first used?
questions.explanation: Nitin Bose directed Dhoop Chhaon, the film in which the playback singing technique was first introduced.

questions.q: Before dedicated playback singing, stars like K. L. Saigal were celebrated in 1930s Indian cinema because they could do what?
options: Act and sing their own songs on screen
questions.explanation: Singing-stars such as K. L. Saigal could both act and sing their own songs, which was essential before playback singing freed actors from this requirement.

questions.q: The National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer was first instituted in India during which decade?
questions.explanation: The National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer was instituted in 1968 (recognising the films of 1967), placing its introduction in the 1960s.

questions.q: Which playback singer, awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2001, recorded songs in numerous Indian languages over a career spanning many decades?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar received the Bharat Ratna in 2001 after a playback career spanning many decades and languages.

questions.q: Playback singing allowed which important change in how Indian films could cast their lead actors?
options: Films could be made entirely without any songs
Actors no longer needed to be trained singers to perform songs
Only opera-trained actors could be cast
Actors had to dub their own dialogue separately
questions.explanation: Because playback singers provided the voice, actors no longer needed singing ability, widening the pool of who could be cast.

questions.q: Which South Indian playback singer, honoured with the Padma Vibhushan, recorded an enormous number of film songs across Telugu, Tamil, Kannada and other languages?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam, awarded the Padma Vibhushan, recorded a vast number of songs across multiple South Indian languages.

questions.q: The Malayalam playback singer K. J. Yesudas is widely known by which honorific title?
questions.explanation: K. J. Yesudas is popularly known as 'Gaanagandharvan', reflecting his stature as a playback singer.

questions.q: Music director Naushad, an influential figure in Hindi film music, frequently worked with which male playback singer in the 1950s and 1960s?
questions.explanation: Naushad had a celebrated collaboration with Mohammed Rafi, producing many memorable songs together.

questions.q: In playback singing, the song is recorded in which order relative to the on-screen filming?
options: The song is recorded after the scene is filmed
The song is pre-recorded before the scene is filmed
The song is recorded at the same instant as filming
The song is never recorded, only performed live
questions.explanation: In playback singing the song is pre-recorded first, and the actor later lip-syncs to it during filming.

questions.q: Which female playback singer, known for her voice in films like Pyaasa and Aar Paar, was married to filmmaker Guru Dutt?
questions.explanation: Geeta Dutt, a prominent playback singer of the 1950s, was married to filmmaker Guru Dutt.

questions.q: Which singer-actor and composer, a key figure at New Theatres, sang and composed for its films in the 1930s era when playback was being pioneered?
questions.explanation: Pankaj Mullick was a leading singer, actor and music director at New Theatres, Calcutta, during the 1930s when the studio pioneered playback singing.

questions.q: Why was the playback technique technically significant when introduced in Dhoop Chhaon (1935)?
options: It allowed songs to be recorded separately and synchronised to picture rather than sung live on camera
It eliminated the need for any musical instruments
It was the first time colour was used in an Indian film
It introduced stereophonic sound to Indian cinema
questions.explanation: Playback's significance was that vocals could be recorded separately in optimal conditions and then synchronised to the picture, rather than sung live during the shot.

questions.q: Which playback singer, born in 1929, recorded songs in over thirty languages and received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1989?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar (born 1929) recorded in over thirty languages and received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1989.

questions.q: The separation of the singing voice from the acting role through playback singing led to a distinctive phenomenon in Hindi cinema where one actor was often 'voiced' by the same singer. Which singer was frequently associated with voicing actor Rajesh Khanna's hits?
questions.explanation: Kishore Kumar became closely associated as the playback voice for many of Rajesh Khanna's hit songs.

questions.q: Which playback singer was famously associated as the 'voice' of actor Raj Kapoor in many of his films?
questions.explanation: Mukesh was widely regarded as the playback voice associated with Raj Kapoor across many of his films.

questions.q: The playback model that emerged after 1935 contrasts with which earlier Indian film, the country's first sound film, where songs were performed by the on-screen cast?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara (1931), India's first sound film, predates playback singing, so its songs were performed by the cast on screen.

questions.q: Among South Indian playback singers, P. Susheela holds a place of distinction recognised for recording a very large number of songs. In which film industries was she most prolific?
questions.explanation: P. Susheela was a leading and prolific playback singer chiefly in Telugu and Tamil cinema.

questions.q: The introduction of playback singing in 1935 is historically attributed to which film studio's technical experimentation, the same studio behind Devdas (1935)?
questions.explanation: New Theatres of Calcutta, also behind the 1935 Devdas, is credited with the technical experimentation that introduced playback singing.
